title: PS-69087 Phase 5: drawing rotation, quick-load PDF, PDF text extraction tests
body:
## Summary

PS-69087 Phase 5 — fills the **None → Direct** test coverage gap for the four FileShare-touching methods called out in `docs/temp/tfs-prep-non-tc-files/test-coverage.md` rows #40, #41, #46, #68:

- `DrawingRevisionRotationHandler.ConvertCoordinatesFromRotatedMatrixToNormal` (#40)
- `DrawingRevisionRotationHandler.ConvertCoordinatesFromNormalMatrixToRotated` (#41)
- `PDFTextHandler.ExtractPDFTextSegmentsToDB` (#46)
- `ProjectSightDMSService.SaveQuickLoadPDFWithAnnotsAndRotations` (#68)

The pre-existing `[Ignore]`'d `UnitTests/DrawingTests/DrawingPageRotationTest` exercised PDFTron primitives rather than the rotation handler itself and was unsuitable as-is, so three new narrow integration tests are added instead.

## What's added

- **`Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Records/Drawings/DrawingRevisionRotationHandlerTests.cs`** — Stages a 90°-rotated drawing PDF in `FMSDirectory` and calls the `internal` `DrawingRevisionRotationHandler.ConvertCoordinates*` methods directly (via the existing `[InternalsVisibleTo]` on `Drawings/PDFTronDocument.cs`). Asserts the rect changes via the page default matrix, via its inverse, and round-trips through both.
- **`Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Records/Drawings/QuickLoadPDFTests.cs`** — Drives the public `ProjectSightDMSService.SaveQuickLoadPDFWithAnnotsAndRotations` path through `DMSSystem.UpdateDMSPdfFileAsync`. Quick-uploads a PDF into `WebFileUploadDirectory`, applies an XFDF rectangle add + a 90° page rotation, and asserts the modified PDF stays in `WebFileUploadDirectory` with a different size. Companion test verifies the missing-temp-file graceful early-return.
- **`Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/PDF/PDFTextHandlerTests.cs`** — Publishes a drawing, clears its `DrawingText` rows, then drives `PDFTextHandler.ExtractPDFTextSegmentsToDB` explicitly and asserts the searchable-text row is created (incl. the appended annotation text). Companion test verifies the missing-DMS-file early-return.
- **`docs/temp/tfs-prep-non-tc-files/coverage-progress.md`** — flips Phase 5 to `[x]` and adds a Phase 5 implementation-notes section mirroring the per-method ↔ test-class table style used by Phase 2 / Phase 3.

Phase 5 playlist entries already exist on the parent branch under the "PDF / annotation export" section of `PS-69087-fileshare-methods.playlist` (rows 40, 41, 46, 68); no playlist edit is required in this PR.

## Test plan

- [ ] Build `ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests` cleanly on a machine with the `TrimbleCloud.FileService` NuGet feed configured (this PR could not be built locally — `NU1101: Unable to find package TrimbleCloud.FileService`).
- [ ] Run the three new test classes via `PS-69087-fileshare-methods.playlist` (or directly):
    - `DrawingRevisionRotationHandlerTests` — 3 tests
    - `QuickLoadPDFTests` — 2 tests
    - `PDFTextHandlerTests` — 2 tests
- [ ] Confirm focused tests pass green; no broad/end-to-end suites need to run for this phase.
author: AdamCollins

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR expands FileShare-touching test coverage by adding new ParallelIntegrationTests around PDF/drawing rotation, quick-load PDF saving, and PDF text extraction, and updates the PS-69087 tracking docs/playlist accordingly.

**Changes:**
- Added new integration tests for `DrawingRevisionRotationHandler` coordinate transforms, `ProjectSightDMSService.SaveQuickLoadPDFWithAnnotsAndRotations`, and `PDFTextHandler.ExtractPDFTextSegmentsToDB`.
- Added additional integration tests for SiteCapture and Trimble Connect reversal scenarios.
- Introduced/updated PS-69087 coverage tracking docs and the test playlist file.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 12 out of 12 changed files in this pull request and generated 10 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| docs/temp/tfs-prep-non-tc-files/PS-69087-fileshare-methods.playlist | Adds/updates a playlist of FileShare-touching test classes, including Phase 5 entries. |
| docs/temp/tfs-prep-non-tc-files/coverage-progress.md | Adds a Phase checklist/progress tracker with Phase 5 marked implemented and notes on method↔test mappings. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/TrimbleConnect/TCMigrationReversalTestsWithoutTC.cs | Adds non-TC tests for `TCMigrationReversalSystem` using substitutes + reflection. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/TrimbleConnect/QuickUploadReversalTestsWithoutTC.cs | Adds tests driving `QuickUploadReversalSystem.FetchFileFromTCAsync` via reflection. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/SiteCapture/SiteCaptureViewer/SiteCaptureViewerImageTests.cs | Adds SiteCapture viewer image/thumbnail staging tests. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/SiteCapture/SiteCaptureStationLinksTests.cs | Adds tests for station thumbnail retrieval paths returning bytes / data URLs. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/SiteCapture/SiteCaptureImageSupportTests.cs | Adds tests for SiteCapture image path helpers and file staging. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/SiteCapture/Processing/CaptureProcessor/ProcessCaptureSystemPositionTests.cs | Adds a failure-path test when a drawing PDF is missing during SiteCapture processing. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Reports/SiteCaptureReportAttachmentTests.cs | Adds a report-generation test ensuring SiteCapture attachments are appended correctly. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Records/Drawings/QuickLoadPDFTests.cs | Adds Phase 5 test coverage for quick-load PDF update/save behavior. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Records/Drawings/DrawingRevisionRotationHandlerTests.cs | Adds Phase 5 test coverage for coordinate conversion between rotated/normal matrices. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/PDF/PDFTextHandlerTests.cs | Adds Phase 5 test coverage for PDF text extraction into `DrawingText`. |
</details>
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

review_comments.body: The PR description focuses on Phase 5 (drawing/PDF coverage), but this PR also adds new SiteCapture integration tests. Please update the PR title/description to reflect the additional scope, or split the SiteCapture tests into a separate PR to keep review and rollout risk focused.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Integration test naming convention in this repo is PascalCase sentences with no underscores. Rename this test method (and the other test(s) in this class) to remove underscores so it matches the established convention.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Integration test naming convention in this repo is PascalCase sentences with no underscores. Rename this test method (and the other test(s) in this class) to remove underscores so it matches the established convention.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Integration test naming convention in this repo is PascalCase sentences with no underscores. Rename this test method to remove underscores so it matches the established convention.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Integration test naming convention in this repo is PascalCase sentences with no underscores. Rename this test method to remove underscores so it matches the established convention.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: This test method name says it validates both Low and High floor-image file names, but the assertions only cover the Low case. Either add an assertion for the High path as well, or rename the test so the name matches what is actually being validated.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Asserting that the PDF file size changed is a brittle proxy for “content updated” (different PDFs can be the same size, and some save paths may preserve size). Consider asserting on a stronger signal such as last-write time, a content hash, or reading back the PDF to verify the rotation/annotation was applied.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: These reflection helpers assume GetMethod() always succeeds; if the private method signature changes, the test will fail with a null dereference that’s hard to diagnose. Add an explicit null check/assert on the MethodInfo (with a helpful message) before invoking it.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: The PR description says no playlist edit is required, but this PR adds/updates the PS-69087 playlist entries (including the Phase 5 rules here). Please update the PR description (or the note in the doc) so it matches what actually changed.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Same as above: this reflection call assumes GetMethod() succeeds. Add a null check/assert on the MethodInfo before invoking so failures are clearer if the private method name/signature changes.
review_comments.author: Copilot

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,431 @@
+<Playlist Version="2.0">
+  <!--

+    <!-- ============================================================ -->

+
+## Phase 0 — Findings & Open Questions
+
+### Resolved by code/build evidence — Prerequisite #1: `DMSSystem.GetFMSDirectory(int projectID)` overload
+
+**Status:** Technically resolved; disposition still requires a team decision.
+
+**Question (from plan):** Confirm whether the unresolved `DMSSystem.GetFMSDirectory(int projectID)` overload referenced by `SearchSystem.SearchPrologWebFolders` (#45) and `IndexProcess.StartWork` (#64) compiles, is dead code, or comes from an external assembly. **Block Phase 8 until resolved.**
+
+**Answer (from build evidence on this branch):** Both call sites are **dead code that does not compile** in the active build. No external assembly defines a `DMSSystem.GetFMSDirectory(int)` overload. There is therefore nothing to test for #45 / #64 in their current form.
+
+#### Evidence
+
+- The only definition of `DMSSystem` in the repo is `Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/DMSSystem.cs` (`public class DMSSystem : SystemBase` in namespace `ProjectSight.DataAccess`). It contains **no** `GetFMSDirectory` member of any signature; the only `GetFMSDirectory` mention inside `DMSSystem.cs` is the call `new ProjectSightDMSService(portfolioDB.RequestedByObject).GetFMSDirectory()` (instance, no-arg) at line 2658.
+- The two existing `GetFMSDirectory` definitions live on `ProjectSightDMSService`:
+    - `public string GetFMSDirectory()` (instance, no args) — `Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/Services/ProjectSightDMS/ProjectSightDMSService.cs:3480`
+    - `public static string GetFMSDirectory(IDBHelper portfolioDB)` — same file, line 3521
+    - **No `(int projectID)` overload exists on `ProjectSightDMSService` or anywhere else.**
+- A whole-repo search for `GetFMSDirectory` finds zero matches under `Common/` (`GCCM.Common.SystemFramework`) and zero matches anywhere outside the two `*.cs` files listed above.
+- `SearchSystem.cs` is **explicitly excluded from compilation** in the only csproj that contains it:
+    - `Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/ProjectSight.BusinessLayer.DataAccess.csproj` line 27 — `<Compile Remove="SearchSystem.cs" />`
+    - No conditional `<Compile Include>` re-adds it elsewhere in the file.
+- `IndexProcess.cs` lives in `Core/Servers/IndexServer/ProjectSight.Servers.IndexServer.csproj`. That csproj is **not referenced from any `*.sln` file** in the repository (`All.sln`, `WebOnly.sln`, `APIOnly.sln`, `ProjectExportServiceOnly.sln`, `ServerMonitorOnly.sln`, `WebUI.sln`, `Office365.sln`, `HPBudgetMapping.sln`, `C1Reporting.sln`, `QATestApp.sln` — none reference `IndexServer`). It also still targets `net4.7` / `LangVersion 5`, consistent with abandoned legacy code.
+- `ITCMigrationReversalSystem.cs` and `Document Search\SearchParameters.cs` are also `<Compile Remove>`'d in `Core/Common/Components/ProjectSight.Common.Components.csproj` (lines 38 and 39), reinforcing that the search/indexing surface is dormant on this branch.
+- The only other consumer of `SearchSystem` is `Core/Web Layer/Unused/DMS/search.aspx.cs` — under the `Unused/` directory.
+
+#### Disposition still needed (lightweight Randy / epic-owner question)
+
+The technical "compiles?" question is answered. What remains is a **policy** call:
+
+> Phase 0 prereq #1 — `SearchSystem.SearchPrologWebFolders` (#45) and `IndexProcess.StartWork` (#64) are confirmed dead code on `wip/adam/tfc-prep-non-tc-files` (and on `lucretia/TrimbleFileService`, which has the same `<Compile Remove>` exclusions): `SearchSystem.cs` is `<Compile Remove>`'d in `ProjectSight.BusinessLayer.DataAccess.csproj`, and `ProjectSight.Servers.IndexServer.csproj` is not referenced from any `.sln`. The `DMSSystem.GetFMSDirectory(int)` overload that both files reference does not exist anywhere in source or in `GCCM.Common.SystemFramework`. **Question for Randy / the search-indexing owner:** Are these methods slated for revival as part of TFS prep, or should Phase 8's #45 / #64 be marked formally deferred in `coverage-progress.md` and removed from the playlist? If revival is planned, when, and which branch?
+
+This question is **non-blocking for Phases 1–7, 9, 10**. It only blocks Phase 8.
+
+### OPEN — Prerequisite #2: `ConnectArchiveSystem.LocatePhysicalLinkedFile` / `LocateFileOrPhoto` scope (#19, #20)
+
+**Status:** Cannot be resolved from code alone. Requires Randy / epic-owner input.
+
+**What code already tells us:**
+
+- Both methods are **private** on `ConnectArchiveSystem` (`Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/ConnectArchiveSystem.cs:267, :292`); only entry point is `FileRecoveryJobHandler` (a background job).
+- They run **only in the TC-as-FMS scenario**: read from `FMSDirectory` / `PhotosDirectory` and push to Trimble Connect.
+- Per `PS-69089-read-methods.md` ("ConnectArchiveSystem — Not Obsolete"), the class is actively called by `FileRecoveryJobHandler`; FileShare reads happen specifically in TCAsFMS scenarios.
+- The per-task notes in `test-coverage.md` and `summary-of-methods.md` flag both as TC-recovery (Medium per the task's "TC-exclusion guidance").
+
+**Question to send to Randy / epic owner:**
+
+> PS-69087 Phase 0 prereq #2 — `ConnectArchiveSystem.LocatePhysicalLinkedFile` / `LocateFileOrPhoto` (test-coverage.md #19, #20) are TC-recovery-only paths reached via `FileRecoveryJobHandler`. They read from `FMSDirectory` / `PhotosDirectory` and push files to Trimble Connect. Per the per-task "TC-exclusion guidance" notes the original triage marked them Medium and out of the main coverage scope, but they are real FileShare reads that will become TFS reads after the migration. Should Phase 8 add a `FileRecoveryJobHandler`-driven test for these (in scope), or formally defer them as TC-only and document the deferral in `coverage-progress.md` (out of scope)?
+
+### OPEN — Prerequisite #3: Minimum supported mobile version (PS-69093 follow-up note 6)
+
+**Status:** Cannot be resolved from code alone. Requires Randy / mobile team input.
+
+**What code already tells us:**
+
+- `Core/API Layer/ProjectSight.WebAPI/Controllers/v1/SyncController.cs` source comments document **introduction** versions, not deprecation versions:
+    - `UploadAttachment` (line 2627) — `// Used in Mobile 2.23 and forward`
+    - `UploadFile` (line 2676) — `// Used in Mobile 2.4x and forward`
+    - `UploadPiecemeal` (line 2720) — `// Used in Mobile 2.23 and forward`
+    - `UploadAppend` (line 2821) — `// Used in Mobile 2.4x and forward`
+- The PS-69093 follow-up note 6 references a stale `USED by 2.38 and older mobiles` comment dated 2026-01-01 that no longer matches what is in source. The current comments do not flag any endpoint as legacy.
+- There is no `MinimumSupportedMobileVersion` config or guard in code that we could use to derive the answer.
+
+**Question to send to Randy / the mobile team:**
+
+> PS-69087 Phase 0 prereq #3 (per PS-69093 write-methods follow-up note 6) — `SyncController.UploadAttachment` (test-coverage.md #99 / PS-69093 row 47) and `UploadPiecemeal` (PS-69093 row 49) are tagged in source as "Used in Mobile 2.23 and forward". The newer pair `UploadFile` (#100) / `UploadAppend` (PS-69093 row 50) is tagged "Used in Mobile 2.4x and forward". What is the current minimum supported mobile client version in production? If the floor is at or above the version that introduced `UploadFile` / `UploadAppend`, the legacy `UploadAttachment` + `UploadPiecemeal` pair (and their `InProgressDirectory` chunking) can be removed, eliminating that surface from the TFS migration. If they must stay, Phase 1 (`Mobile/LegacyUploadsRetrievalTests.cs`) should add coverage for both endpoints rather than just the `uploads://` legacy path.
+
+This question is **non-blocking for Phase 1 implementation** — Phase 1 already plans `Mobile/LegacyUploadsRetrievalTests.cs` against `ExternalFileSystem.ConfirmLegacyFileInUploadsDirectoryAsync`. The mobile-version answer changes the *breadth* of Phase 1 / Phase 10, not the start gate. Phase 8 is the only phase explicitly gated.
+

+
+- **`TCMigrationReversalSystem.cs` is also `<Compile Remove>`'d** in `Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/ProjectSight.BusinessLayer.DataAccess.csproj` (line 28), and `ITCMigrationReversalSystem.cs` is `<Compile Remove>`'d in `Core/Common/Components/ProjectSight.Common.Components.csproj` (line 38). On this branch, all of `TCMigrationReversalSystem.DownloadFileAsync` / `DownloadAndUpdateDMSFileAsync` / `FetchNonPersistedQueuedFileProperties` (test-coverage.md #90, #91, #54, #53) are uncompiled. **Phase 2 must verify the file is back in the build on whichever branch Phase 2 targets** before its tests can run; the canonical compile-include is presumably restored on `lucretia/TrimbleFileService` when the migration code lands. Phase 2 agent: confirm build on target branch first.
+- The `<Compile Remove>` block also lists `MaterialSystem.cs`, `FACSystem.cs`, and `ERPSystemAvro.cs`, none of which are in the PS-69087 method list — included here for completeness only.

+## Phase 5 — Implementation notes (branch `wip/adam/PS-69087-phase5-coverage`)
+
+Direct (None → Direct) coverage for the four methods in test-coverage.md rows #40, #41, #46, and #68. The pre-existing `[Ignore]`d `UnitTests/DrawingTests/DrawingPageRotationTest` exercised PDFTron primitives rather than the rotation handler itself, so it was unsuitable as-is and a new narrow integration test was written instead.

+| 41 | `DrawingRevisionRotationHandler.ConvertCoordinatesFromNormalMatrixToRotated` | `Records/Drawings/DrawingRevisionRotationHandlerTests` — `ConvertCoordinatesFromNormalMatrixToRotatedTransformsSquareRectViaInverseMatrix` + the round-trip test |

+| 68 | `ProjectSightDMSService.SaveQuickLoadPDFWithAnnotsAndRotations` | `Records/Drawings/QuickLoadPDFTests` — `QuickLoadPDFWithAnnotationChangesUpdatesFileInWebFileUploadDirectory`, `QuickLoadPDFWithMissingTempFileReturnsWithoutError` (driven through public entry point `DMSSystem.UpdateDMSPdfFileAsync`) |
+
+Both `DrawingRevisionRotationHandler` methods are `internal static`. Test assembly access is provided by the existing `[assembly: InternalsVisibleTo("ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests")]` declaration in `Drawings/PDFTronDocument.cs`.
